prompt:
Our services emit logfmt lines and we currently both produce and parse them with hand-rolled strings.Split garbage scattered across four repos. Yesterday the alert pipeline silently dropped a page because a message contained an equals sign. I'm consolidating this into one real codec package and pointing everyone at it.

Package logfmt, root of module go-logfmt, stdlib only:

  type Field struct { Key string; Value any }
  func Encode(fields []Field) (string, error)
  func Decode(line string) ([]Field, error)
  type SyntaxError struct { Pos int; Msg string }   // implements error

Encoding:
- One line, fields joined by single spaces, keys in the exact order I hand them over — do not sort, order is meaning in our pipelines.
- Accepted value types: string, int, int64, float64, bool, time.Duration (rendered via its String(), so 90s comes out 1m30s). Anything else, including nil: error.
- Floats via strconv.FormatFloat 'g' -1. Bools as true/false.
- Strings encode bare when they can; quote with double quotes when the value is empty or contains a space, '=', '"', '\', or control chars. Inside quotes escape exactly: \\ , \" , \n , \t.
- Keys: non-empty, no spaces, '=', '"', '\', or control characters — otherwise error.

Decoding (the parse side of the same dialect):
- Runs of spaces separate fields; leading/trailing spaces and empty lines are fine. Duplicate keys are legal and stay in order.
- key=value where an UNQUOTED value gets typed by inference, tried in this order: true/false -> bool; base-10 int -> int64; float -> float64; time.ParseDuration -> time.Duration; otherwise string. A QUOTED value is always a string, no re-typing — "true" the string must survive.
- Bare key with no '=' at all decodes as the flag true. key= with nothing after is the empty string.
- Malformed input gets a *SyntaxError with Msg and Pos = 0-based byte offset: empty key (offset of the '='), unterminated quote (offset of the opening quote), unknown escape (offset of the backslash), junk glued after a closing quote (offset of the junk), a '"' in the middle of a bare value (offset of the quote).
- Encode then Decode preserves field order and values whenever the wire representation carries the type unambiguously. Ambiguous bare forms intentionally follow Decode's inference: int normalizes to int64; an integral float64 may decode as int64; and a bare string matching bool, integer, float, or duration syntax decodes to that inferred type. Do not add type markers, alter FormatFloat output, or quote beyond the stated string rules to preserve Go dynamic types.

logfmt_test.go is the contract, leave it be. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... green means done.
